steps.title: Rewrite the second equation to isolate the known ratio
steps.content:
The second equation $\frac{84j}{14xk} = 58$ can be rewritten by factoring out $\frac{j}{k}$:
$$\frac{84j}{14xk} = \frac{84}{14x} \cdot \frac{j}{k}$$
This separates the unknown variable $x$ from the ratio we know.

steps.title: Substitute the known value and simplify
steps.content:
Since we're told $\frac{j}{k} = 58$, substitute this into the rewritten equation:
$$\frac{84}{14x} \cdot 58 = 58$$
Divide both sides by 58:
$$\frac{84}{14x} = 1$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
From $\frac{84}{14x} = 1$, multiply both sides by $14x$:
$$84 = 14x$$
Divide by 14:
$$x = \frac{84}{14} = 6$$
C is correct.
